fix: use import type for TypeScript interfaces

Rolldown (Vite 8 bundler) correctly rejects value imports of
TypeScript-only interfaces. Switch to 'import type' so the bundler
can eliminate them at compile time and not fail at runtime.

commit.message:
docs: fix and complete API Reference with all actual endpoints

Replaced inaccurate API Reference with comprehensive table of all endpoints:

Endpoints now documented:
- /get/schema - Get command schema
- /get/version - Get build version
- /get/status - Get server status
- /get/token - Get security token
- /post/run - Execute command
- /get/global - Get entire global JSON
- /get/global/path - Get value at JSON path
- /post/global - Replace entire global JSON (requires token)
- /post/global/patch - Apply merge patch
- /post/global/persist - Force save to disk
- / - Serve web GUI
- /* OPTIONS - CORS preflight

Added accurate documentation for:
- Authentication requirements (token for certain endpoints)
- Query parameters (e.g., ?path=/a/b)
- Request body formats
- Response structures
- Error codes with HTTP status mappings
- curl examples for each endpoint

commit.message:
feat: MCP server + A2A Agent Card support (with all blocking fixes)

* feat: add MCP server + A2A Agent Card support

- shared/mcp_server.py: FastMCP server exposing all 3 agents as tools
- api/app.py: mount /mcp endpoint + /.well-known/agent.json A2A card
- requirements.txt + pyproject.toml: add mcp[cli]>=1.3.0 dependency
- tests/test_mcp_server.py: unit tests for MCP tools and A2A card
- docs/mcp-a2a.md: integration guide for MCP clients and A2A
- CHANGELOG.md: document v1.5.0 release

* feat: expose agents via MCP and A2A

- mount FastMCP server at /mcp
- add /.well-known/agent.json A2A card
- add mcp dependency and bump version to 1.5.0
- update changelog for MCP/A2A release

* docs: update README for MCP and A2A support

* fix(mcp): set streamable_http_path='/' to avoid /mcp/mcp double-prefix

FastMCP by default registers its handler at /mcp inside the sub-app.
When mounted at /mcp the full path became /mcp/mcp. Setting
streamable_http_path='/' makes the endpoint accessible at /mcp.

Also set raise_server_exceptions=False in TestA2AAgentCard fixture:
MCP session manager requires asyncio lifespan context (run()) which
is not available in unit tests, causing RuntimeError instead of 500.

* fix(mcp): optional import, /mcp auth guard, _invoke_agent error handling, revert list_jobs rename

- api/app.py: make MCP import optional (try/except ImportError) so API starts
  without mcp[cli] installed; conditional app.mount('/mcp', ...); revert
  list_jobs_endpoint → list_jobs (unnecessary rename)
- api/app.py: add _mcp_auth_guard middleware — returns 401 when API_KEY is set
  and /mcp request has no valid X-API-Key / Authorization header (A2A Agent
  Card at /.well-known/agent.json stays public per A2A spec)
- shared/mcp_server.py: wrap agent.invoke() in try/except — return structured
  error dict {output:'', steps:0, error:str(exc)} instead of unhandled exception
- tests/test_mcp_server.py: add tests for error handling and /mcp auth guard

* fix(review): address PR #25 Copilot review comments

- api/app.py: error branch now stores metadata-only request_payload
  (same pattern as success path: no content_base64 in JSONB)
- api/app.py: extract _format_created_at() helper (handles PG datetime,
  ISO-strings and None) and use it in _check_and_process + check_duplicate
  instead of raw str(created_at)[:10]
- api/app.py: _mcp_auth_guard now checks _MCP_AVAILABLE before enforcing
  auth, so /mcp returns 404 (not 401) when mcp package is not installed
- shared/mcp_server.py: use logger.exception() in _invoke_agent error
  handler to preserve full traceback in production logs
- README.md: document /mcp auth behaviour (API_KEY-conditional) in
  endpoint table with footnote; fix footnote placement (after table)
- CHANGELOG.md: fix mixed quotes «API" → «API»

* fix(mcp): add input size guard to MCP tools

Add _MCP_MAX_INPUT_CHARS = 5_000_000 constant (matching REST API's
ProcessRequest.text max_length) and validate chat_input length in
_invoke_agent before dispatching to LLM. Returns structured error dict
if input exceeds limit, preventing excessively expensive calls from
authorized MCP clients with very large payloads.

Also add test coverage for the new guard.

* fix(review): address new PR #25 Copilot review comments

- shared/mcp_server.py: move _MCP_MAX_INPUT_CHARS size check to the top
  of _invoke_agent, before agent creation — avoids expensive LLM/graph
  initialization for inputs that will be rejected anyway
- api/app.py: add 'Authorization' to CORS allow_headers so browser/JS
  MCP clients can use 'Authorization: Bearer' without preflight failure
- api/app.py: make Authorization header parsing robust — case-insensitive
  'bearer' scheme, strip() whitespace; use str.partition instead of
  removeprefix which is scheme-case-sensitive
- api/app.py: agent_card() now reads X-Forwarded-Proto + Host headers
  (nginx passes both) so /.well-known/agent.json returns correct
  https:// url when behind reverse proxy instead of http://
- tests/test_mcp_server.py: patch create_dzo_agent and assert_not_called
  in the large-input test, verifying the guard fires before agent init

* fix(review) + feat(noobkeys): address PR #25 comments, add noobkeys backend

PR review fixes:
- api/app.py: extract _attachment_meta() helper — correct size_bytes
  calculation accounting for base64 '=' padding (was len*3//4, now
  len*3//4 - count('=')); used in both success and error paths so
  they never diverge
- shared/mcp_server.py: fix MCP_LOG_LEVEL in __main__ — normalize to
  .upper(), validate with isinstance(int), set level on logger directly
  (basicConfig alone has no effect after setup_logger adds handlers)
- CHANGELOG.md: fix Latin 'c' -> Cyrillic 'с' in 'с описанием'

noobkeys backend:
- config.py: add 'noobkeys' to _VALID_BACKENDS
- shared/llm.py: add _NOOBKEYS_BASE_URL constant; handle noobkeys in
  build_llm() — uses OPENAI_API_KEY + https://noobkeys.onrender.com/v1
  (overridable via OPENAI_API_BASE); update docstring
- .env.example: add noobkeys configuration section
- tests/test_llm.py: 5 new tests for noobkeys backend (default URL,
  custom URL override, retries with/without fallback, missing API key)

* fix(noobkeys): max_tokens 8192→4096 (Groq free tier TPM limit)

* fix(review): OPTIONS preflight bypass in _mcp_auth_guard + test_ui_config .env isolation

- api/app.py: _mcp_auth_guard now skips auth check for OPTIONS requests so
  CORS preflight works correctly when API_KEY is set (browser/JS MCP clients)
- tests/test_ui_config.py: patch dotenv.load_dotenv in TestLlmBackend.test_default
  to prevent .env file (LLM_BACKEND=noobkeys) from leaking into test env after
  monkeypatch.delenv — fixes flaky test regression from noobkeys backend addition

* feat(llm): switch GitHub Models to new endpoint models.github.ai/inference

- shared/llm.py: update _GITHUB_MODELS_BASE_URL to https://models.github.ai/inference
  (old https://models.inference.ai.azure.com migrated by GitHub)
- shared/llm.py: update _GITHUB_MODELS_PRIORITY to gpt-4.1, gpt-4.1-mini, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini
  (tested: all support tool-calling, 8000 token limit, 32768 max output tokens)
- tests/test_llm.py: update base_url assertions to new endpoint

Manual test results on https://models.github.ai/inference:
  openai/gpt-4.1:      ✅ tool-calling, ~8000 token max input, 32768 output
  openai/gpt-4.1-mini: ✅ tool-calling, ~8000 token max input
  openai/gpt-4o:       ✅ tool-calling, ~8000 token max input
  openai/gpt-4o-mini:  ✅ tool-calling, ~8000 token max input

* fix(auth): GITHUB_TOKEN takes priority over OPENAI_API_KEY for github_models backend

When LLM_BACKEND=github_models and OPENAI_API_KEY contains a non-GitHub key
(e.g. noobkeys key), the old 'effective_openai_key() or GITHUB_TOKEN' order
caused 401 Unauthorized because the noobkeys key was sent to GitHub Models API.

Fix: swap priority to 'GITHUB_TOKEN or effective_openai_key()' so the env-native
GitHub token is always preferred. OPENAI_API_KEY remains a fallback for
environments where a GitHub PAT is stored there and GITHUB_TOKEN is not set.

Also: add test_github_token_takes_priority_over_openai_api_key covering this case.

* fix: address code review issues from PR #25

- api/app.py: guard negative size_bytes in _attachment_meta (max(0, ...))
- api/app.py: fix /mcp path check to avoid matching /mcpfoo paths
- api/app.py: validate X-Forwarded-Proto in agent_card (http/https only)
- shared/llm.py: fix misleading comment about max_tokens_out (not TPM)

* Update shared/llm.py

* fix(mcp_server): disable logger.propagate in stdio mode to prevent duplicate log output

In __main__ stdio block, setup_logger() has already added handlers to the
named 'mcp_server' logger but left propagate=True.  When logging.basicConfig()
later installs a root handler, every log record from 'mcp_server' ends up in
both its own handlers AND the root handler — doubling each line.

Fix: set logger.propagate = False before calling basicConfig so the named
logger's records stay in its own handlers while root-logging still serves
third-party dependencies (mcp, uvicorn, etc.).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/OlegKarenkikh/dzo-tz-agents/sessions/9526c0b1-8eee-4b70-a9b6-657618b793c9

Co-authored-by: OlegKarenkikh <44174021+OlegKarenkikh@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(mcp): resolve 6 blocking issues — event loop, CORS, registry, job tracking, middleware, proto

1. Event loop blocking: MCP tool functions are now async, using
   asyncio.to_thread() to offload sync agent invocations to the
   thread pool — prevents blocking /health, /metrics during LLM calls.

2. CORS on 401: _mcp_auth_guard now attaches CORS headers to its 401
   response so browser-based MCP clients see the real error instead of
   an opaque CORS failure.

3. Single AGENT_REGISTRY: MCP list_agents() and A2A Agent Card skills
   now derive from AGENT_REGISTRY (api/app.py) instead of hardcoded
   duplicates.

4. MCP validation pipeline: MCP tool calls now go through
   _create_mcp_job() which creates tracked jobs in the database —
   making them visible in /api/v1/jobs, /api/v1/stats, Prometheus.

5. Middleware ordering: Added comprehensive documentation of the
   middleware execution order and the CORS-header requirement for
   _mcp_auth_guard.

6. Additional: X-Forwarded-Proto logs warning on invalid values,
   MCP_LOG_LEVEL gracefully warns on invalid input, A2A protocolVersion
   updated to 0.2.1.

commit.message:
[chore](build) Add code style enforcement documentation, skills, and clang-tidy script (#62307)

Problem Summary:
The AGENTS.md and Copilot skills had no documentation about code style
enforcement workflows for BE (C++ clang-format/clang-tidy) or FE (Java
checkstyle). This made it unclear how and when to run formatting and
static analysis tools before committing code changes.

This PR adds:
1. A "Code Style Enforcement" subsection under "Coding Standards" in
AGENTS.md covering BE clang-format, BE clang-tidy, and FE checkstyle.
2. Three new skills: `be-code-style`, `fe-code-style`,
`clang-tidy-check`, each providing step-by-step procedures for fixing
style issues.
3. A new `build-support/run-clang-tidy.sh` script that runs clang-tidy
incrementally on **only changed lines** (not entire files) by parsing
git diff, avoiding noise from pre-existing code.

### Release note

None

### Check List (For Author)

- Test: Manual test
- Verified check-format.sh, run-clang-tidy.sh (--help, --files, --base,
--full, line-level filtering logic), and FE checkstyle all work
correctly
- Behavior changed: No
- Does this need documentation: No (this IS documentation)

Bug fixes:
- Force-approval: each reviewer agent now checks if it's actually a required
  reviewer for the PR before posting a force-approval comment. Previously all
  3 agents (PE, PM, Architect) would post redundant force-approvals even when
  only 2 were needed for merge.
- Conflict warnings: check existing PR comments before posting duplicate
  conflict detection warnings. PR #317 had 6 identical warnings from 6 commits.
- TE coverage signal: added CheckTestCoverageCompleteAsync() that sets status
  to 'coverage met, tests complete' when all merged code PRs are tested. The
  HealthMonitor picks this up to emit testing.coverage.met, unblocking the
  Testing→Review phase transition. Previously this signal was NEVER emitted.

commit.message:
feat(seo): migrate previousSlugs to locale-aware previousSlugsByLocale

PROBLEM: previousSlugs was a flat array with no locale context. When a
job's FR slug changed but the old slug matched the active DE slug, the
safety nets removed it — causing 404s for /fr/trouver-emploi-tessin/<old-slug>.

SOLUTION: New locale-aware format previousSlugsByLocale: { fr: [...], de: [...] }
with per-locale safety nets that only strip matches within the same locale.

Core changes:
- 6 helper functions in dedicated-crawler-common.mjs:
  addPreviousSlugForLocale, getPreviousSlugsForLocale, getAllPreviousSlugs,
  cleanPreviousSlugsPerLocale, syncLegacyPreviousSlugs, mergePreviousSlugsByLocale
- Rewrote captureLostSlugs() to write per-locale
- Fixed all 4 safety nets (hardenJobLocaleFields, writeJobsCrawlerSlice,
  regenerate-slugs-by-locale, cleanPreviousSlugsPerLocale)
- Fixed all write points (mergePreserveLocaleData, preferJob)
- Fixed build plugin bridge page generation (locale-aware)
- Fixed runtime consumers (JobBoard.tsx, router.ts, localeJobsSplitPlugin)
- Updated scatter-jobs, find-orphan, assemble-jobs-dataset

Data migration:
- Recovered 106 previousSlugs lost in commit 7723f77f1 cleanup
- Migrated 3680 flat previousSlugs to locale-aware format across 111 files
- Legacy flat previousSlugs kept in sync for backward compatibility

* fix: normalize warning_pct to 2 decimal places for lossless UI round-trip

The frontend form uses integers 0-100 but the backend stores 0-1 decimal.
Values with excess precision (e.g., 0.3333) would lose data on edit via
Math.round(). Adding a Pydantic field_validator with ROUND_HALF_UP on both
LimitCreate and LimitUpdate ensures the stored value always maps cleanly
to a whole percentage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: draw continuous average trend line across sparse historical data

Use null instead of omitting the average property so Recharts
connectNulls draws a continuous dashed line through gaps. Also guard
the tooltip against null values to avoid showing misleading \.

Adds backend test documenting _average_series behavior with empty
periods (months with zero expenses are excluded from the average).

fix(configure-live): create /var/usrlocal before using /usr/local

/usr/local is a symlink to ../var/usrlocal (bootc layout). bootc-rootfs.sh
wipes /var so the symlink target is missing. mkdir -p through a broken
symlink returns exit 1. Recreate /var/home and /var/usrlocal at script start.
